data.title: As a DSO maintainer, I bake DSO infrastructure + project-name placeholders + tickets orphan branch
data.description:
## Description

What: Commit pre-filled DSO infrastructure files, add project-name placeholders aligned with the installers substitution patterns, and push a tickets orphan branch so the installer has everything it needs for zero-interview bootstrap.
Why: Walking skeleton - makes the template DSO-ready. Without this the installer clones a generic NextJS app with no /dso:roadmap support.
Scope:
- IN: Commit CLAUDE.md, project-understanding.md, design-notes.md, .claude/ARCH_ENFORCEMENT.md, dso-config.conf (with dso.plugin_root per installer contract), .pre-commit-config.yaml, .github/workflows/ci.yml pre-filled for non-engineer NextJS prototype; add project-name placeholders at every substitution point covered by scripts/create-dso-app.sh; create and push tickets orphan branch with an empty initial commit.
- OUT: NOTICE (S4); end-to-end validation (S5); interface contract audit (S6); documentation updates (S7).

## Done Definitions

- When complete, all 7 DSO infrastructure files are present and contain no TODO, TBD, or FILL-IN markers in content-requiring sections.
  Satisfies: Fully pre-populated DSO infrastructure files (SC3)
- When complete, the set of project-name placeholders in the template matches the set of substitution patterns in scripts/create-dso-app.sh sed logic - cross-checked by grepping both the template and the installer. Any new substitution point requires a matching change in both. (Applied from adversarial review F1.)
  Satisfies: Project-name substitution works end-to-end (SC5)
- When complete, git ls-remote against the template repo for the tickets branch returns a ref.
  Satisfies: tickets orphan branch pushed to the remote (SC1)
- When complete, a user running the installer with name demo-proto produces a scaffolded project where grep across the project returns zero project-name placeholder matches outside .git/ and package.json name equals demo-proto.
  Satisfies: SC5
- When complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic.

## Considerations

- [Testing] Substitution set audited against create-dso-app.sh sed patterns before closing. (F1)
- [Shared State] dso-config.conf plugin_root contract: read detect_dso_plugin_root() in scripts/create-dso-app.sh - if the installer expects an empty value to fill post-clone, ship empty; if the installer expects a plugin-root placeholder, ship placeholder. Document the chosen approach in the template README so future maintainers do not diverge. (F7)
- [Accessibility / Maintainability] Follow .claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md for infrastructure file formatting.

## Escalation Policy

Escalation policy: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue - one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
